game.title: Slush
game.short_text: A time looping platformer

details.title: Slush
details.description:
Full screen the game so that the UI isn't cut off. A download is available if the web build has a reduced frame rate
The world is stuck in a time loop, and you have to stop the clock to try and escape,  get to the end quickly or else you'll be forced back to the beginning.!
The faster you are, the higher your score - comment your score below :)
Controls:
A/D to move left / right
Space to jump
K to spawn a platform

game.title: Evade - Jam version
game.short_text: Dodge and control time to avoid obstacles.

details.title: Evade - Jam version
details.description:
THE FINAL VERSION CAN BE PLAYED IN CRAZYGAMES IN THIS LINK.Left mouse click to use time / ESC to pause.
Control TIME and gain power ups for each wave completed. To complete a wave, you need to survive a certain TIME. This game was made for  Gamedev.js Jam 2023 with the theme TIME in a week.
A game made by gubebra and mikomoares.

Flash Revival Challenge
Game inspired by Avoider. Avoider is also a mouse avoider type game. The minimalistic and simple colors were inspired by the game. Three of the six enemies types in Evade were also inspired by the flash game (circle bounces the window, arrow shoots torwards player and triangle moves like the rockets from the game). Avoider enemy function in phases, that we decided to portrait as waves. Last but not least, Evade is a hypercasual and simple game to play rigt away.
Open Source Challenge
Jam code on Github. The game is made entirely in shaders, with no assets/sprites whatsoever.

Music by BjoKib. 
SFX1, SFX2, SFX3, SFX4, 
If you are having low fps issues, make sure hardware acceleration is turned on for your browser. That’s probably the fix.

game.title: Potion Panic
game.short_text: See if you can keep calm while handling orders, brewing potions, earning money and buying upgrades in Potion Panic!

details.title: Potion Panic
details.description:
Brew potions, handle orders and manage your own potion shop! Can you keep track of everything as the shop evolves as you play?
The Grand Wizard is going on a well deserved vacation, and has put you in charge of running his potion shop. To make sure you do your job, he has cast a hex on you, so you can't stop moving!
Adventurers need potions for their quests, so now you must brew their potions and manage the shop. The potion recipes are very delicate so be careful! If you miss a step, you will have to wait for the brew to evaporate before you can try again.
The shop is full of delicate magical artefacts and ancient tomes. Try not to break anything!
Controls:Move the apprentice with the mouse.
Press 'Left-Click' to throw ingredients and potions. This is useful when you are in a rush to deliver a potion, or you need to discard an unwanted ingredient.
Press 'P' to pause the game when you need a break.

NOTE: At the moment, the performance of the web build for Mac OS is suboptimal. If you are using a Mac, we recommend playing in windowed mode with reduced graphic settings, or using the downloadable build.

Features:
This game consist of approximately 30-40 minutes of gameplay to gain all unlocks. 

Throw ingredients and potions

Mix different ingredients and make potions

Sell your potions to questing adventurers and earn money


Buy new recipes and upgrades 



  
    Enjoyed Potion Panic?
    Experience the skies as Gizmo Rabbit in our vehicle-building simulation game.
  



Credits:
Potion Panic is a game jam game developed by Dream Jam Games. We are a small party of hobbyist game developers, participating in occasional game jams, whenever possible.
 Our games each seek to deliver a joyous experience, delivered in  small packages of easily ingestible little pieces  - Like delicious jars  of jam.
 So grab a spoon, load it up with one of our games and dig in!
The following members took part in the creation of Potion Panic:https://ladane.itch.io/https://troldeansigt.itch.io/https://duendue.itch.io/https://dennisdovendyr.itch.io/


Third Party Assets:
Font: Motley Forces Regular - 1001 Fonts
Arrow Graphics:Image by juicy_fish on Freepik 
Sounds:All sounds are from Freesound.org
Music:Main Menu Theme - Puzzle Game by Viacheslav_Okhrymenko, pond5.comGame soundtrack - Electro Swing Fun by SunlightAudio, pond5.com
High Res Images in picture frames and at Game Over screen:Generated with chatbot


Thanks for playing!

game.title: nostalgia
game.short_text: In the darkness of an abandoned box, an old television set gains self-awareness and new, interesting skills.

details.title: nostalgia
details.description:
In the darkness of an abandoned box, an old television set gains self-awareness and new, interesting skills. Will nostalgia be enough to prevent him from a sad fate? "Nostalgia" is a rhythm game prepared for Gamedev.js Jam 2023.



The game supports two modes:
Story mode - RECOMMENDED: Play this mode if you want to have fun and enjoy the story without facing a difficult challenge that will require multiple attempts.
Challenge mode: Can you keep up with the rhythm and master the game? Play this mode if you want a higher level of challenge!
During the creative process, I was inspired to interpret the theme of the Game Jam "Time" in a unique way. The result is a play where the main character is transported back in time through an encounter with an ancient artifact. While remaining in the present, the protagonist is able to witness a past time and experience a different era first-hand.
I invite you to share your feedback and scores in the comments below. Your input allows me to continually improve and enhance this game for an even better gaming experience. Thank you for playing!

game.title: Yurei - The Spirit
game.short_text: A Wizard uses the Yurei spell to conjure sprits to rewind & control time

details.title: Yurei - The Spirit
details.description:
As the Wizard, your ultimate goal is to escape the 7 levels of Hell Underground by finding and collecting 3 keys. However, the journey to freedom won't be an easy one, as each level is filled with traps & puzzles.
To help you on your quest, you possess the "Yurei" (幽霊) spell, which allows you to rewind and control time. After you cast the spell, you have a few seconds to act, and your Yurei or Spirit will mimic the actions you performed when time rewinds. This can be a valuable tool to help you avoid traps and solve puzzles.


Timing and positioning are crucial when using the Yurei spell. You must carefully plan your actions and decide when and where to cast the spell to ensure that your Yurei will perform the actions you need to progress through the level.
As you progress through each level, the challenges and obstacles become more difficult. Only by using your wit, strategy, and magic can you hope to escape the depths of Hell Underground and claim your freedom.
Are you ready to take on the challenge and escape the 7 levels of Hell Underground as the Wizard?
------------------------------------------
Developed for Gamedev.JS Jam 2023. Theme is  TIME.
Arrow Keys to MoveSpace to invoke Yurei spellR to restart a level

game.title: Time Rush
game.short_text: Time Rush is a GameDevjs Submition

details.title: Time Rush
details.description:
Ah, Time Rush! My  submission to the game Jam, brought to life in just five days. Now, I know what you're thinking: "Five days? Weren't you given two whole weeks?" Well, in my defense, I didn't exactly join the party on time. You see, I completely missed the memo until a full week had already passed me by. But hey, that's what caffeine-fueled coding binges are for, am I right? With a little sleep deprivation and a lot of determination, I managed to churn out Time Rush in record time. Who needs a full two weeks, anyway? I'll take my five-day masterpiece any day of the week!
The Android got a small bug :)
Game concept
The game concept revolves around the jam's theme of "Time," where Time Rush emphasizes that time is the ultimate currency. Every aspect of the game, including ammunition, health terminals, and the timer, are all interconnected, and using any of them consumes time while providing a beneficial outcome. Whether it be replenishing ammo for your weapons, repairing your level, or unlocking the portal at the end of each level, each action requires a careful balance of time management to progress through the game.
Terminals

Pick Ups
Like most games, Time Rush offers a variety of pickups to help players heal and protect themselves. Throughout the levels, you will come across time capsules that heal your timer by five units, thereby also healing yourself. Additionally, you will encounter shield capsules that enable you to ignore a single instance of damage, with the number of capsules you collect directly influencing how many instances of damage you can ignore. With these pickups at your disposal, you can carefully strategize and maneuver through each level, minimizing the damage taken while optimizing your use of time to progress through the game.

Ah, the age-old struggle of a game developer: too many ideas, too little time. I could have taken Time Rush to the next level, with all sorts of crazy features and gameplay mechanics. Alas, pesky old work kept getting in the way, and my time was running short. But just in case anyone's curious, here are a few of the ideas
 
Boss 2 second faze

Boss 3

Skills that I wanted to add (I added only the shield)

Wow, if you're still reading this, you deserve a medal! Seriously though, from the bottom of my heart, thank you for taking the time to play Time Rush. I know there are a million and one other things you could be doing right now, so the fact that you've chosen to spend even a small chunk of your day on my little game means the world to me.

And fear not, my dear reader, for Time Rush is far from over. I'll be continuing to work on the game and document my progress through regular devlogs, so you can stay in the loop and watch as this little project of mine grows into something truly special.

Of course, I couldn't have done any of this without the amazing folks over at GameDevjs, who provided the perfect platform for me to showcase my skills and creativity. This game jam was my first, but it certainly won't be my last. So thanks again, and I hope to see you in the next one!


Assets Used
wibuka - Trevor-pupkin - bdragon1727


THANK YOU AGAIN

game.title: Time Match
game.short_text: Match the digital clock's time with the analog clock.

details.title: Time Match
details.description:
Description
It's time for analog to fight back!
The evil digital clocks have left their home galaxy of the Digital Nebula and come to invade the peaceful Analog Star Cluster.
Choose one of three analog heroes to represent you in the fight against digitization.
A special targeting system has been developed by the Analog War engineers. To use it you must match the time on the incoming digital clock, using the hour and minute hands. Once they match, a special homing missile will be fired to destroy the evil digital tyrant.
But be careful, if the digital clock strikes the analog clock it will loose one of it's three lives.
Score high and you will be rewarded with your own spot on the galactic score board.
May the seconds be with you!
The Goal
 Time match is intended to be an educational game. In this digital age, the opportunity for children to learn analog time is low. 
Time Match seeks to teach kids how to associate analog time with it's digital equivalent in a fun manner. Enhance pattern matching skills by giving control of the hour and minute hand to the player. As well as develop quick recall by adding a time based incentive.
Devices
This version of Time Match, the itch.io version, is intended to be played on Desktop using the keyboard. There are also UI controls, the plus and minus buttons, if the player wishes to use the mouse.
Keyboard Controls



Hour Hand




Up Arrow Key
Increment Hour


Down Arrow Key
Decrement Hour






Minute Hand




Right Arrow Key
Increment Hour


Left Arrow Key
Decrement Hour



The Team
Brian Douglas - ProgrammerEmma Douglas - Graphic DesignerAndres De La Espriella - Sound and Music Designer

game.title: Save Your Time
game.short_text: A shooter game

details.title: Save Your Time
details.description:
Source code
Save Your Time
This game is made for the Gamedev.js Jam 2023
Intro
In a world where time is everything, a powerful clock at the center of the universe is the only source of time. However, an army of greedy monsters want to steal time from the clock.
As the controller of the clock, you are the last line of defense against an never-ending invasion of time-wasting monsters that threaten to steal time and create chaos in the universe. With your quick reflexes and precise aiming, you fire powerful time vortexes from the clock's hands to defeat the monsters that are approaching from all directions.
As you progress through the game, you will encounter more challenging monsters that require different strategies to defeat. You'll need to carefully choose which powerups to use and when to fire to maximize your effectiveness and save your time.
Will you be able to save the clock from endless wave of monsters? The fate of the world depends on your skills and bravery. Enter the world of "Save Your Time!" and find out!
How to Play
How to Survive
In this game, you control a clock and monsters will come at you from all directions. Your objective is to stay alive by shooting them. A timer will be displayed and it will decrease second by second, and if it runs out, you lose the game. The only way to lengthen the timer is to kill monsters.
Shooting
To shoot enemies, you can left-click on the screen or press the SPACE key. The projectiles will come out from each hand of the clock.
Aiming
Aiming with keyboard
You can aim by adjusting the time on the sidebar. Alternatively, you can use the keyboard to adjust the time. If you look at your numpad, you can imagine the 4, 5, 6, 1, 2, 3 keys to be aligned with the arrows on the sidebar. To use this shortcut, you must have the num lock on. Q, A, W, S, E, D keys are also an option and they are aligned with the arrows on the sidebar.
Aiming with mouse
You can aim by adjusting the time with up and down button on the top right side in Controls. The buttons are dark blue. Up buttons stand for increasing time by 1 for whatever you press in hour/minute/second. Down buttons stand for decreasing time by 1.

Rewards
Shooting down enemies will earn you rewarded time. Each enemy holds a different value of reward. The closer the enemy gets to you, the lower the reward will be.

game.title: Survival of the Flippest
game.short_text: A game of second chances

details.title: Survival of the Flippest
details.description:
Death has finally come for you, hourglass in hand, as the final seconds of your life pour away... But wait! He's left the hourglass out where you can reach!
Keep flipping the hourglasses to buy yourself more precious time and see how many seconds you can extend your life by. How many hourglasses could he have?
Don't ask yourself if the time you gained was well spent flipping hourglasses again and again. Don't ask yourself if you're wasting your life now playing this game as actual real seconds of your life pass never to be recouped. Just keep flipping!Music by PJ Kimber (Spotify)

game.title: Latency
game.short_text: A Packet Manager has a stressful job

details.title: Latency
details.description:
You are a Packet Manager, trying your best to sort the packets to their correct ports! Run around, throw some data, and enjoy the CHAOS.
HOW TO PLAY: 
You must get the colored packets into their respective ports to reduce latency. Keep your total latency (red plus green numbers) below 100, or else you lose! Packets that are sitting out will also start decaying, and after 30 seconds they'll disappear. They also count for less time the longer they are out so move move move!
CONTROLS:
WASD/Arrows Move
Space       Jump
E           Pickup Packets
F           Throw Packets
R           Reset Level
ESC         Main Menu

game.title: Birds!
game.short_text: Help the OVERLORD to find his birds lost in space!

details.title: Birds!
details.description:
Help the mighty overlord in five missions to find his birds in space. Estimate how long it will take for you to get the birds and the overlord will make you an offer. If you make it you will get the reward, but if not you need to pay the overlord!
Try to beat my highscore: 26265 CZD
My submission to Gamedev.js Jam 2023!Challenges I participated for this jam:
Open Source: Code is available on GitHub.Overlord's Arena: Used assets and some lore from OVERLORD.
Controls
Touch / click the screen or press SPACE.
How to Play
Complete all 5 missions and get rich! Every mission consists of the following steps:
Estimate: Have a look at the map, estimate how long you will need to get the birds and get an offer from the overlord.Flying: Take your spaceship and collect all birds. Ensure you make it in time and do not crash into space debris.
Scoring
You get paid in CREEPZ DOLLARZ!
When you complete a mission within the time you estimated you get the reward the overlord offered you. For every crash you had during the mission you will loose 100 CZD. If you do not make it within the estimated time you need to pay the overlord half of what he offered instead and try again.
Tips and Tricks
When the spaceship is idle and rotating (at the start and after a crash) you can clearly predict in which direction it will move when you start again. The ship will turn in the same direction as it is currently rotating.If the ship is rotating clockwise, the left engine will turn on and it will move clockwise.If the ship is rotating counter clockwise, the right engine will turn on and it will move counter clockwise.The lower the time you estimate is, the more the overlord will offer (and vice versa).Setting the time so high that you for sure make it, will result in a low offer and not much CZDs. You do not get more CZDs if you finish before the time you estimated. You will always get what the overlord offered you before the mission (minus the crash fee).Setting the time too low will increase the chance of failure and you will loose CZDs.Estimating just the right time, not too low to be able to make it and not too high to get a good offer is key to become rich!
Credits and Tools
GitHub PageFramework: Phaser 3
Code: Home made typescript spaghetti code using WebStormBundler: vite.js
Graphics: Hand painted and adapted assets from OVERLORD using Aseprite
Sound: Almost :) all by myself using Reaper, Focusrite Scarlett 2i2 interface, MODO DRUM plugin, AmpliTube 5 plugin, a Gibson SG Special and a vacuum cleaner.Bird chirping in the music was taken from taavhaap from Freesound (license).Bird chirping when collecting was taken from InspectorJ from Freesound (license).

details.title: The Darkest Hour
details.description:
The Darkest Hour
A legends says that deep down in this ruins a secret can be found. A secret such worthy that told by many. But tried by few. A risky venture with a terrible curse for the ones who try. A curse trapping them forever, never letting them to return. Becoming something dark and vile. A loss of light, endless darkness. A creature of dark, endless dweller of the ruins.
After a daylong exploration of a dungeon underneath the ruins of an old abandoned castle, you ought to return to the surface. As the night slowly falls outside, you come to realize the rooms you have been wandering suddenly feel different. Is someone or something watching? The suspense has grown to the point it can physically be felt.
The lantern you have been carrying has been running out of oil and there is no much time before everything becomes completely dark. You must leave the place now. Or this might be the darkest hour of your life...
Info
Playing on a larger screen (e.g. PC) with headphones is recommended!
Made for: https://itch.io/jam/gamedevjs-2023
Source code, art and sound: https://github.com/lorentzo/ribiz-jam
Controls
ActionBindingMoveW A S D / Arrow KeysRunShift
Team
lovbos: Art & Graphicsbornabesic: Scripting & SoundLeo: Level Design
Technologies
Godot Engine: Game DevelopmentBlender: Art, Assets, Environment Art, Characters and AnimationKrita: TexturesLMMS: MusicAudacity: Processing of Sound FX

game.title: Capy's Timeporium
game.short_text: Puzzle game about a watchmaking Capybara

details.title: Capy's Timeporium
details.description:
Capy inherited a watchmaking business from his grandfather. Now he has to follow his family's legacy.
This is a puzzle game, click on one of the cat's clocks to start a puzzle, drag the gears to the correct spots to make them all connect!
Game made for Gamedev.js Jam 2023

game.title: Lone Knight: To The Past
game.short_text: The demon slime has become too powerful. Take a journey to the past and defeat it when it's still weak.

details.title: Lone Knight: To The Past
details.description:
Hi there, thanks for your visit.
"The Demon slime has become too strong to handle. Make a quick trip to the past and slay it when it's weak."

Defeat all the enemies you meet, level up, collect items, and finally face the future demon slime before the guardian of time notices your action.

Keyboard Controls:

A / D => Move Left / Right
Space => Jump
E => Inventory
P => Pause

Mouse: 

Primary (Left) => Attack
Secondary (Right) => Dash

External Asset:

Some of the sound effects: KenneyMusic: Alkakrab, SVL

game.title: Tix
game.short_text: A rhythm game made for gamedev.js 2023

details.title: Tix
details.description:
Tix is a rhythm game made as part of Gamedev.js game jam 2023. Navigate a treacherous clockwork factory to find the mischievous Tix the Cat. Just make sure to keep your movements in time and not raise the suspicion of the factory's security systems.
Tix is open source. Its source code can be found here:
https://github.com/Liam-Hegarty/tix
https://gitlab.com/heggroup/tix
